D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Michelson Stellar Interferometer — Star Diameter via Visibility

Two mirrors separated by baseline B sample the star's spatial-coherence function; first visibility zero at B = 1.22 λ/θ gives the angular diameter directly

Van Cittert-Zernike theorem: spatial coherence of starlight at the ground is the Fourier transform of the brightness distribution. Michelson (1920) measured Betelgeuse's diameter (47 mas) with a 6 m baseline at 575 nm. CHARA array reaches sub-mas resolution today.

Slide baseline B; fringes wash out at first visibility zero.
